[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [22°36′27.55″S 17°5′27.50″E﻿ / ﻿22.6076528°S 17.0909722°E﻿ / -22.6076528; 17.0909722](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Independence_Stadium_(Namibia)&params=22_36_27.55_S_17_5_27.50_E_type:landmark)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

National stadium of Namibia

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/Independence_Stadium_Windhoek_Namibia_Main_Gate.jpg/250px-Independence_Stadium_Windhoek_Namibia_Main_Gate.jpg)](/wiki/File:Independence_Stadium_Windhoek_Namibia_Main_Gate.jpg)

Main gate of the Independence Stadium in 2019

For other uses, see [Independence Stadium](/wiki/Independence_Stadium_(disambiguation) "Independence Stadium (disambiguation)").

The **Independence Stadium** in [Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek")'s Olympia suburb is the [national stadium](/wiki/National_stadium "National stadium") of the Republic of [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"). Owned by the [Government of Namibia](/wiki/Government_of_Namibia "Government of Namibia") it holds 25,000 spectators and is mainly used for [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") events.

As of 2021[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Independence_Stadium_(Namibia)&action=edit), the stadium has been described as "dilapidated". The [Confederation of African Football](/wiki/Confederation_of_African_Football "Confederation of African Football") (CAF) has decommissioned the stadium in 2021 for it being sub-standard. No other Namibian stadium meets CAF's requirements. As a result, international games of the [Namibia national football team](/wiki/Namibia_national_football_team "Namibia national football team") will have to be played abroad.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The stadium hosted the wake of President [Hage Geingob](/wiki/Hage_Geingob "Hage Geingob") in February 2024,[[2]](#cite_note-2) and hosted a memorial service for Namibia's first president [Sam Nujoma](/wiki/Sam_Nujoma "Sam Nujoma") in February 2025.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
